message:
fix(http): respect handler-set Content-Length in HEAD responses per RFC 9110 §9.3.2

src/http/h1/server.rs — HEAD response Content-Length synthesis now only
triggers when the handler did not already declare a Content-Length header.
Previously, the condition would overwrite an explicit handler-set
Content-Length when body_len was nonzero, violating RFC 9110 §9.3.2 which
requires the HEAD response to carry the same Content-Length that would
appear in the equivalent GET response. When the handler sets Content-Length
explicitly, it is trusted as the authoritative GET length.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
